name: Quincy Jones
abstract: Quincy Delight Jones Jr. (March 14, 1933 – November 3, 2024) was an American record producer, composer, arranger, record executive, conductor, trumpeter, film and television producer, and bandleader.

interviews.title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: CityKids Speak at Carnegie Hall... Voices from the Front Line

works_created.t: I Needs to Be Bee’d With

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Let’s talk about your work with Quincy on Give Me the Night... So I joined him. I joined his band on that show... we started talking about making a record.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: So I introduced him to Quincy Jones and he… Quincy did his number… So he put together… he selected all the musicians on there. He put together all of those wonderful charts on there… Working with Quincy was one of the great experiences for me
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: We did, he did “The Wiz,” the movie “The Wiz,” which was huge sessions of many, many musicians. In fact on one session I played tuba along with Dave Bargeron, Don Butterfield and Tony Price.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'I’m trying to learn how to play trumpet, and I would sure like to take some lessons from you.' ... I said, 'Yeah, okay, all right.' He’d come by every morning and take his lesson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerome Richardson
about_quotes.evidence: The Quincy Jones band was a great one... we were together from beginning to end about ten months... I told Quince I said, 'Why don't you do it in six?' So he did it in six
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerome Richardson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: So I joined Quincy’s band with two rehearsals... The trombone section was a very, very good section with Åke Persson playing lead, Melba Liston, Curtis Fuller, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
